title: Syracuse parking garage closes due to structural concerns
summary: The parking garage at 300 East Fayette Street in downtown Syracuse has closed due to structural deterioration identified during an engineering inspection. The 41-year-old facility served 100-150 daily parkers including city staff and private monthly users. The city is directing users to the Washington Street parking garage as an alternative while safety concerns are addressed.

political_signal.description: City prioritizing public safety over operational convenience; proactive communication about infrastructure vulnerability and contingency planning.

processing_notes: Article date shows November 6, 2025 publication but metadata shows November 7, 2025 — minor inconsistency
No budget impact figures provided; cost of repairs and revenue loss unknown
No timeline for reopening or remediation plan disclosed
Relates to broader downtown infrastructure and asset management concerns relevant to capital planning
